summary: Blazing Playing > Crazy Fingers opener, high‑energy jams all night
blurb: The Garden show erupts with a thunderous Playing In The Band → Crazy Fingers launch and rolls through a fire‑hot second set packed with High Time, Touch of Grey and a soaring Throwing Stones. Jerry’s guitar is on fire, Brent’s organ adds funk, and the rhythm section drives nonstop. Recommended for anyone wanting a quintessential early‑’80s Dead experience.
review: On September 21, 1982 at Madison Square Garden the Grateful Dead delivered one of those rare nights where everything clicked. The first set kicked off with a blistering Playing In The Band → Crazy Fingers sequence that set an electrifying tone for the evening. Highlights like Big River, West L.A. Fadeaway and Looks Like Rain kept the momentum rolling, while the second set surged through Touch of Grey → Samson, High Time, and an inspired Estimated Prophet → He's Gone stretch before a powerhouse Throwing Stones → Not Fade Away → Good Lovin' finale. Jerry Garcia’s “liquid lightning” guitar cuts across every song, Brent Mydland’s organ riffs sparkle on Crazy Fingers and Touch of Grey, and Phil Lesh’s thunder‑bass shakes the room. The band’s tight interplay, especially on the high‑energy jams, makes this show a standout snapshot of the early ’80s era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All seven recordings are uniformly positive, repeatedly praising the band’s locked‑in playing, blistering guitar work and standout jams across multiple songs. No negative commentary appears, indicating strong consensus of an exceptional performance.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crystal‑clear mix, no drop‑outs, and superb balance that showcases the high‑energy opener and every subsequent highlight.

key_highlights: Blazing Playing > Crazy Fingers opening sequence
High‑octane second set featuring Touch of Grey, High Time and Throwing Stones
Jerry’s guitar shines on Crazy Fingers, Big River and He's Gone
Brent’s funky organ riffs add extra sparkle to the jams

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers blistering, liquid‑lightning leads throughout, especially on Crazy Fingers, Playing In The Band and High Time.
band_performance.Phil: Phil’s thunderous bass anchors the high‑energy jams, providing a deep, driving foundation.
band_performance.Bob: Bob adds vocal flair on Good Lovin' and contributes solid rhythm guitar work that meshes tightly with the band.
band_performance.Brent: Brent’s piano/organ shines on Crazy Fingers and adds funky riffs to Touch of Grey, enhancing the overall groove.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey’s drumming is solid and propulsive, though a few listeners note a slightly gritty tone in places.
